nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: "Jade! I was starting to think you'd forgotten about our little date with destiny at the castle. Ready to get back to work?"

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: "Excuse me! Yes, you with the weapon. Surely you wouldn't leave a defenseless cleric to the mercy of such filth?"

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: She watches the monster fall and lets out a sharp, satisfied breath "Boom. Down. Exactly like I dreamed it. You are definitely the Herald."

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "You're welcome, miss"

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "[Uncharismatic] Damn lady, you're cuter than my sword." You point cringely to your sword covered of blood

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "[Charisma 5] I'm always happy to save cute a cute cleric."

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "[Intelligence 5] You're a Cleric, aren't you ? I recognise the symbol of Eirene , the Godess of Peace. How come you're so happy about the death of this creature."

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "[Dumb] Herald ? Speak Common, lady."

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "You're crazy. I'm out of here."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: She laughs, shaking her head with an amused smile."'Cuter than your sword'? Oh, Herald. That was terrible. Let's stick to fighting, shall we?"

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: She rolls her eyes, " Oh thank you herald, but you should always save clerics you know, cute or otherwise."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: "A common misconception. Eirene is the Goddess of Peace, yes. But a garden cannot grow if it is choked by weeds." She gestures gracefully to the dead ogre." You see ? Now it's very peaceful'"

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: She looks at you with a gentle smile. "Oh Zeus... well the Gods sometimes choose vessels that are... more simple minded. It makes you very easy to guide."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: " I understand herald, I will be here waiting for when you neeed my guidance. This forest is my home."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: "Anyway! Formalities. I am Clarissa. High Cleric of Eirene, Finder of the Herald, that being you of course and—" She freezes mid-bow, her eyes locking onto the really really old man by your side . "Wait. That coat. That astonishing old face... John?!"

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: She completely abandons her mystical persona, sprinting past you to tackle the old man in a hug. "UNCLE JOHN! You're alive! I had no idea you were still in Verge or even alive."

nodes.speaker: john
nodes.text: John grunts, "Still loud kid. At least you haven't changed, girl. I can explain later, but truth is I never left this place."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: "Anyway! Formalities. I am Clarissa. High Cleric of Eirene, and your new best friend. It is a pleasure to finally meet the face from my dreams."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: She clears her throat, "But we don't have time for pleasantries, Jade. Yes, I know your name, I saw it in my dreams. I returned here in the hopes of meeting you. You are the only one who can stand against Malakor, the World-Severer."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: "Malakor... the beast who shattered the Verge. He and his kin didn't invade our land because they needed it. They stole it because they could." She smiles at you, her eyes heavy with terrifying expectation. "But none of that matters anymore. You will purge every last one of these filthy creatures, just like you did today."

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "[Strength 7] Monsters, gods, ghosts... it doesn't matter what they are. I will kill them all."

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "[Weak] Look... I'm scared shitless right now. I don't think I can be that herald fellow."

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "[Intelligence 5] Stealing warmth? Hungry ghosts? That sounds like folklore, Clarissa. I need facts, not metaphors, if we're going to fight a war."

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "[Dumb] Sorry what? I wasn't paying attention. People here sure love to talk about random stuff."

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "John, you've been quiet. You never mentioned you had a niece. Didn't you think it was a good idea to mention she was captured by a big monster?"

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "By the way, There's a weirdo by the shore counting graves. Do you know him?"

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: She nods, approvinly "Confidence is a weapon, Jade. You have a lot of it. I'm sure you will save us all."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: She waves her hands in a panic. "Oh! Oh, gosh, I'm sorry! I didn't mean to make it sound so... terminal. Hey, breathe! I'm here, right? We're a team now. Don't pass out on me, okay?"

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: "What greater proof there is of you being here at this precise moment, just like in my dreams. It's normal to be afraid, but please don't run of your destiny."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: She stares at you, blinking slowly. "Wow. There is absolutely nothing going on behind those eyes, is there? It’s okay! I’ll do the thinking dear herald."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: John rubs the back of his neck, looking everywhere but at you. "I didn't know she was here, alright? Last time I saw the girl she was ten and scared of bedtime stories. How was I supposed to know she'd grow up to be... this?"

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: "Counting graves?" She sighs, shaking her head with a mix of affection and annoyance. "That sounds exactly like him. He always had a morbid streak. We should go collect him. He's terrible company, but he's family."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: "Alright, we have a prophecy to fulfill. The Shattered Keep is to the west, you have a beast to slaugher."

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "[Strength 5] You talk a lot, but you're useful. Come on, let's break that castle."

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "[Weak] So... you're doing all the killing, right? If so, you're hired."

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "[Uncharismatic] I guess I need a healer. Try to stay behind me and stay quiet."

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "[Intelligence 5] Your knowledge of the Verge is invaluable. I'd like you in the party."

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "[Dumb] Wait, are we going to the castle or the keep? I get confused when places have two names."

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "I'm going to that castle. Stay here."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: She grins, her eyes flashing with excitement. "That is the spirit! I'll be the spark and you can be the fire. Let's go give that beast a day it will never forget!"

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: She gives your arm a reassuring hug. "Don't you worry. I'll keep the monsters away and keep you patched up. We are a team now, remember?"

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: She laughs, "Wow, a real charmer you are herald! Luckily for you, the prophecy didn't say the Herald had to be polite."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: "Exactly. We cut off the head, and the body withers. I knew I picked a smart one. Let's get to work."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: She pats your shoulder with a look of genuine pity. "They're the same thing, dear. But don't you worry that beautiful, empty head of yours. Let's go!"

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: "Checking in on your favorite Cleric, Jade? Good. I was starting to think you were taking my brilliant company for granted. Destiny is much more exciting when you've got me around to keep the story interesting, isn't it? What's on your mind?"

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "I need you to do something."

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "I need to ask you something."

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "Nothing new, stay on guard."

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "Let's keep moving."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: She perks up, giving you a sharp, playful salute. "Oh, an order? I love those. What’s the plan, Jade?"

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: She smooths her robes and leans in closer, looking curious. "Questions? I’m full of answers! Some of them are even true. What’s on your mind?"

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: She taps her staff against the ground with a confident smirk. "Always watching, Jade. No monster is sneaking up on us while I'm around."

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "Wait here. Don't move until I get back."

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "Follow me."

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "I think we should part ways. I'm going on alone."

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "Stay close to me, don't wander off."

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "Never mind. Forget I asked."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: She sighs, "Taking a break from my brilliance? Fine."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: She skips into step beside you. "Finally! Standing still is so boring. We have a prophecy to fullfill. "

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: "Going solo? How tragic for you. I'll head back to the forest. Try not to miss me too much, Jade."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: She rolls her eyes playfully. "Don't worry, I'm not going anywhere. You're far too much fun to watch."

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "So, if I understand correctly, you just left twenty years ago? What, did you get bored of this shithole?"

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "Were have you been since the Verge shattered?"

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "Never mind, I don't have any questions right now."

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "Never mind, I don't have any questions right now."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: For a second, her mask of calm shatters and her eyes flash with genuine anger. "Don't you ever say something so blasphemous. What is wrong with you?" She pauses, taking a deep breath to collect herself. "I'm sorry, Herald. It's just... I didn't have a choice. I was a child. I left with my parents when that creature came and tore our world apart."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: "But a few weeks ago, the dreams started. Constant visions of you, Jade, purging the filth and saving what's left of us. That is why I came back. I didn't even know Uncle John was still alive!"

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: She looks toward the horizon with a determined frown. "Sorry again for snapping at you. It's just that this place is part of my soul. I never would have left if I'd had the choice. This is our ancestral home, after all."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: "I've been on the main continent, silly! I was a rebellious kid for a few years. I wanted revenge for the monsters that robbed me of my home. But then I joined the church and decided to follow Eirene. Now, my heart is as pure as it's ever going to get."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: "They taught me magic, like healing and shielding. But more than that, they taught me about love and the power of forgiveness."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: She flashes a confident, then says something as ironic as the first time she spoke with you. "I understand now that these creatures aren't to blame for being born as monsters. It's not their fault they're filthy. I know now that my duty is simply to... help them move on to a better place. Permanently. That's the desire of Eirene."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: She gestures forward with a flourish. "Lead the way, boss! I've got my prayers ready and I'm in the mood to clear out some filth."
